key_highlights: S&P 500 hits record, best month since 2020, driven by Big Tech earnings and AI optimism.
Fed holds rates steady, citing Iran war-driven inflation; Powell remains on board amid DOJ probe.
Oil prices surge to 4-year high ($4.30/gallon gas) due to Iran stalemate and UAE quitting OPEC.
Big Tech earnings (Meta, Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet) show mixed results; Meta falls on rising capex, while AI data center orders surge 500%.
Blue Owl's SpaceX bet boosts earnings, easing private credit fears; KKR launches $10B AI firm.
Yen soars on suspected Japan intervention; dollar has worst month since June.
Geopolitical risks escalate: Iran war, UAE leaves OPEC, Ukraine hits Russian refinery.
Consumer resilience noted by Hyatt CEO, but gas prices and mortgage rates rise.
Regulatory actions: bipartisan bill to ban Chinese auto parts, CFPB finalizes small lending data rule, Maryland bans surveillance pricing.
AI legal battles: OpenAI trial (Musk vs. Altman), Meta charged in Europe over child safety.

sectors.trend: Mixed earnings with strong AI capex; Meta falls on spending concerns, while data center orders surge 500% for some firms. Apple earnings are a sideshow as new CEO prepares.
sectors.implications: AI infrastructure buildout remains a key driver, but investors demand proof of ROI. High capex may pressure margins near-term, but long-term AI adoption supports growth.

sectors.trend: Oil prices surge to 4-year high ($4.30/gallon gas) due to Iran war, UAE leaving OPEC, and Ukraine hitting Russian refinery. Gas prices hit new highs.
sectors.implications: Inflationary pressure mounts, potentially delaying Fed rate cuts. Energy stocks benefit, but consumer discretionary and transport sectors face headwinds.

sectors.trend: Blue Owl surges on SpaceX bet, easing 'doomsday' fears for private credit. Fed holds rates steady, but dissents put rate cuts in doubt. Citi retreats from physical metals.
sectors.implications: Private credit remains resilient, but rate sensitivity is high. Bank earnings mixed; regional banks may face pressure from higher rates and regulatory changes.

sectors.trend: Consumer resilience noted by Hyatt CEO, but gas prices and mortgage rates rising. Chipotle tests happy hour tacos to win cautious consumers. Costco makes rare change to hot dog combo.
sectors.implications: Lower-end consumers are feeling the pinch from inflation, while higher-end remains strong. Retailers may need to offer value to maintain foot traffic.

sectors.trend: Bipartisan bill to ban Chinese vehicles and auto parts. Stellantis reports Q1 results. United Airlines flight reports drone strike. Gas prices surge impact logistics.
sectors.implications: Supply chain shifts away from China could benefit domestic automakers but raise costs. Drone incidents highlight aviation safety risks. Higher fuel costs pressure margins.

sectors.trend: Two medical stocks skyrocket after beating views. Bristol Myers reports surprise sales growth. Novo-backed Avalyn soars 44% in IPO. Kymera Therapeutics reports earnings.
sectors.implications: Biotech IPO market shows signs of life. Drug pricing and regulatory environment remain key risks, but innovation drives upside.

sectors.trend: NNN REIT reports Q1 earnings. Mortgage rates rise due to oil spike. Italy's Meloni backs housing access plan. Miami mayor discusses growth and wealth influx.
sectors.implications: Higher mortgage rates may cool housing demand, but commercial REITs with long-term leases remain stable. Miami continues to attract capital.

sectors.trend: Treasuries draw buyers after selloff as oil surge stalls. Yen soars on suspected Japan intervention. Dollar has worst month since June. ECB officials see June hike if energy costs don't ease.
sectors.implications: Bond yields may stabilize if oil prices plateau. Yen intervention signals currency volatility. Dollar weakness supports emerging markets but may fuel inflation.

key_themes: AI infrastructure spending boom and ROI scrutiny
Geopolitical risk: Iran war, UAE OPEC exit, Ukraine strikes
Inflation resurgence driven by energy prices
Central bank policy divergence: Fed hold, ECB hawkish, Japan intervention
Regulatory crackdown on tech, auto, and consumer finance
Private credit and alternative investments gaining traction
US-China decoupling in auto and tech supply chains

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Geopolitical escalation (Iran war, UAE OPEC exit, Ukraine strikes)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase exposure to energy and defense stocks; reduce exposure to consumer discretionary and transport. Hedge with commodities and inflation-linked bonds.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Inflation resurgence due to oil prices
risk_assessment.mitigation: Shift portfolio to value stocks, energy, and real assets. Reduce duration in fixed income. Consider TIPS and floating-rate notes.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Fed policy uncertainty (rate hold, dissents, DOJ probe on Powell)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Maintain diversified bond ladder; avoid long-duration bonds. Monitor Fed communications closely. Prepare for potential rate hikes if inflation persists.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Big Tech capex overshoot without ROI
risk_assessment.mitigation: Focus on companies with clear AI monetization paths (e.g., Microsoft, Amazon). Reduce exposure to high-capex names like Meta. Consider AI infrastructure plays like KKR's new firm.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory risk (Chinese auto ban, CFPB rule, Meta charges, OpenAI trial)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify across geographies and sectors. Avoid overconcentration in Chinese auto supply chain. Monitor legal outcomes for tech giants.
Hedge currency exposure in international portfolios. Consider long yen positions if intervention continues. Favor emerging markets with strong fundamentals.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase exposure to AI infrastructure and data center plays
opportunities.rationale: Data center orders surge 500%, KKR launches $10B AI firm, and Big Tech capex remains high. Companies like KKR, Blue Owl (SpaceX bet), and suppliers benefit.

opportunities.recommendation: Add energy and defense stocks to hedge geopolitical risk
opportunities.rationale: Oil prices at 4-year high, UAE leaves OPEC, Ukraine strikes Russian refinery. Energy stocks benefit from higher prices. Defense stocks gain from increased tensions.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider biotech IPOs and beaten-down AI stocks
opportunities.rationale: Avalyn soars 44% in IPO, two medical stocks skyrocket. Cathie Wood buys $18M of beaten-down AI stock. Biotech and AI offer high-risk/high-reward opportunities.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in US domestic manufacturing and auto parts
opportunities.rationale: Bipartisan bill to ban Chinese vehicles and parts will boost domestic suppliers. Companies like DR Horton (housing) and Sonic Automotive may benefit from reshoring.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary and transport
defensive_moves.rationale: Gas prices at $4.30/gallon and rising mortgage rates pressure lower-end consumers. Chipotle testing happy hour tacos signals caution. Transport costs rise.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against inflation with TIPS and commodities
defensive_moves.rationale: Oil surge and Fed hold suggest inflation may persist. TIPS provide direct inflation protection. Commodities like gold and copper benefit from geopolitical uncertainty.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce duration in fixed income; favor short-term bonds and CDs
defensive_moves.rationale: Best CD rates up to 4.15% APY. Treasuries draw buyers after selloff, but rate uncertainty remains. Short-term bonds offer yield with less price risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Monitor and reduce exposure to Meta and high-capex tech
defensive_moves.rationale: Meta shares fall on rising capex, investors need proof of spending payoff. $96B bond sale indicates debt reliance. Consider trimming positions until ROI clarity.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Markets are likely to remain volatile due to geopolitical tensions (Iran, UAE OPEC exit) and inflation concerns. The S&P 500's record run may pause as oil prices pressure margins and consumer spending. Big Tech earnings will continue to drive sentiment, but high capex and regulatory risks (Meta charges, OpenAI trial) could weigh. The Fed's hold and dissents suggest no rate cuts soon, supporting a 'higher for longer' rate environment. Energy and defense sectors should outperform.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: AI infrastructure buildout remains a secular growth driver, but ROI scrutiny will increase. US-China decoupling in auto and tech will reshape supply chains, benefiting domestic manufacturers. Inflation may moderate if oil prices stabilize, but geopolitical risks persist. Emerging markets could benefit from dollar weakness. The Fed may eventually cut rates if inflation eases, but not before late 2026. Private credit and alternative assets will continue to attract capital.
key_catalysts: Iran war developments and potential Hormuz reopening
UAE's final OPEC exit and impact on oil supply
Big Tech earnings follow-through and AI capex ROI
Fed's next meeting and Powell's testimony
US election cycle and regulatory actions
watch_list: Oil prices (WTI, Brent) and gasoline prices
Big Tech stock performance (META, MSFT, AMZN, GOOGL, AAPL)
Consumer confidence and retail sales data
Geopolitical headlines from Iran, Ukraine, and China
